verses.1: I am the man who has seen affliction
verses.2: He has led me and caused me to walk in darkness,
verses.3: Surely he turns his hand against me
verses.4: He has made my flesh and my skin old.
verses.6: He has made me dwell in dark places,
verses.7: He has walled me about, so that I can’t go out.
verses.8: Yes, when I cry, and call for help,
verses.9: He has walled up my ways with cut stone.
verses.10: He is to me as a bear lying in wait,
verses.11: He has turned away my path,
verses.13: He has caused the shafts of his quiver to enter into my kidneys.
verses.14: I have become a derision to all my people,
verses.15: He has filled me with bitterness.
verses.16: He has also broken my teeth with gravel.
verses.17: You have removed my soul far away from peace.
verses.19: Remember my affliction and my misery,
verses.21: This I recall to my mind;
verses.22: It is because of the LORD’s loving kindnesses that we are not consumed,
verses.24: “The LORD is my portion,” says my soul.
verses.25: The LORD is good to those who wait for him,
verses.26: It is good that a man should hope
verses.27: It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.
verses.28: Let him sit alone and keep silence,
verses.29: Let him put his mouth in the dust,
verses.30: Let him give his cheek to him who strikes him.
verses.31: For the Lord will not cast off forever.
verses.33: For he does not afflict willingly,
verses.34: To crush under foot all the prisoners of the earth,
verses.35: to turn away the right of a man before the face of the Most High,
verses.36: to subvert a man in his cause, the Lord doesn’t approve.
verses.37: Who is he who says, and it comes to pass,
verses.38: Doesn’t evil and good come out of the mouth of the Most High?
verses.39: Why should a living man complain,
verses.40: Let us search and try our ways,
verses.41: Let’s lift up our heart with our hands to God in the heavens.
verses.43: “You have covered us with anger and pursued us.
verses.44: You have covered yourself with a cloud,
verses.45: You have made us an off-scouring and refuse
verses.46: “All our enemies have opened their mouth wide against us.
verses.47: Terror and the pit have come on us,
verses.48: My eye runs down with streams of water,
verses.52: They have chased me relentlessly like a bird,
verses.53: They have cut off my life in the dungeon,
verses.55: I called on your name, LORD,
verses.57: You came near in the day that I called on you.
verses.58: Lord, you have pleaded the causes of my soul.
verses.59: LORD, you have seen my wrong.
verses.60: You have seen all their vengeance
verses.61: You have heard their reproach, LORD,
verses.62: the lips of those that rose up against me,
verses.63: You see their sitting down and their rising up.
verses.64: You will pay them back, LORD,
verses.65: You will give them hardness of heart,
verses.66: You will pursue them in anger,
